ruvector-complete_240.title: Core Attention
ruvector-complete_240.content:
| Mechanism | Complexity | Memory | Best For |
|-----------|------------|--------|----------|
| **DotProductAttention** | O(n^2) | O(n^2) | Basic attention for small-medium sequences |
| **MultiHeadAttention** | O(n^2*h) | O(n^2*h) | BERT, GPT-style transformers |
| **FlashAttention** | O(n^2) | O(n) | Long sequences with limited GPU memory |
| **LinearAttention** | O(n*d) | O(n*d) | 8K+ token sequences, real-time streaming |
| **HyperbolicAttention** | O(n^2) | O(n^2) | Tree-like data: taxonomies, org charts |
| **MoEAttention** | O(n*k) | O(n*k) | Large models with sparse expert routing |

ruvector-complete_251.title: Graph Attention
ruvector-complete_251.content:
| Mechanism | Complexity | Best For |
|-----------|------------|----------|
| **GraphRoPeAttention** | O(n^2) | Position-aware graph transformers |
| **EdgeFeaturedAttention** | O(n^2*e) | Molecules, knowledge graphs with edge data |
| **DualSpaceAttention** | O(n^2) | Hybrid flat + hierarchical embeddings |
| **LocalGlobalAttention** | O(n*k + n) | 100K+ node graphs, scalable GNNs |

ruvector-complete_260.title: Specialized
ruvector-complete_260.content:
| Mechanism | Type | Best For |
|-----------|------|----------|
| **SparseAttention** | Efficiency | Long docs, low-memory inference |
| **CrossAttention** | Multi-modal | Image-text, encoder-decoder models |
| **NeighborhoodAttention** | Graph | Local message passing in GNNs |
| **HierarchicalAttention** | Structure | Multi-level docs (section -> paragraph) |

ruvector-complete_269.title: Hyperbolic Math Functions
ruvector-complete_269.content:
For Poincare ball embeddings - curved space that naturally represents hierarchies:

| Function | Description | Use Case |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| `expMap(v, c)` | Map to hyperbolic space | Initialize embeddings |
| `logMap(p, c)` | Map to flat space | Compute gradients |
| `mobiusAddition(x, y, c)` | Add vectors in curved space | Aggregate features |
| `poincareDistance(x, y, c)` | Measure hyperbolic distance | Compute similarity |
| `projectToPoincareBall(p, c)` | Ensure valid coordinates | Prevent numerical errors |

ruvector-complete_283.title: SONA - Self-Optimizing Neural Architecture
ruvector-complete_283.content: SONA enables AI systems to continuously improve from user feedback without expensive retraining:

ruvector-complete_287.title: Features
ruvector-complete_287.content:
- **Two-tier LoRA**: MicroLoRA (rank 1-2) for instant adaptation, BaseLoRA (rank 4-16) for long-term learning
- **EWC++**: Elastic Weight Consolidation prevents catastrophic forgetting
- **ReasoningBank**: K-means++ clustering stores and retrieves successful reasoning patterns
- **Lock-free Trajectories**: ~50ns overhead per step with crossbeam ArrayQueue
- **Sub-millisecond Learning**: <0.8ms per trajectory processing

ruvector-complete_775.title: v0.1.0 - 2025-11-19
ruvector-complete_775.content:
**Phase 1: Foundation**
- Core vector database implementation with redb storage
- Memory-mapped vector access via memmap2
- SIMD-optimized distance metrics

**Phase 2: HNSW Indexing**
- HNSW graph implementation
- Parallel index construction using rayon
- Zero-copy serialization with rkyv
- Scalar quantization (int8) for 4x memory compression

**Phase 3: AgenticDB Compatibility**
- Full AgenticDB API implementation with 5-table schema
- Reflexion Memory, Skill Library, Causal Memory Graph
- 9 RL algorithms support

**Phase 4: Advanced Features**
- Product Quantization (8-16x compression)
- Filtered Search
- Hybrid Search (vector + BM25)
- MMR (Maximal Marginal Relevance)

**Phase 5: Multi-Platform Deployment**
- Node.js Bindings (napi-rs)
- WASM Module (wasm-bindgen)
- CLI Tool

**Phase 6: Performance Optimization**
- AVX2 and ARM NEON SIMD support
- Lock-free data structures
- Cache-optimized layouts
- Arena allocators

ruvllm-complete_34.title: Overview
ruvllm-complete_34.content:
RuvLLM is a **self-learning LLM orchestration system** that combines:

- **SONA** (Self-Optimizing Neural Architecture) for adaptive learning
- **HNSW** (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) graphs for efficient memory
- **FastGRNN** for intelligent model routing
- **SIMD** acceleration for high-performance inference
- **LoRA/EWC** for continual learning without catastrophic forgetting
- **Federated Learning** for distributed, privacy-preserving training

ruvllm-complete_45.title: Key Features
ruvllm-complete_45.content:
| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Self-Learning** | Automatically adapts from interactions via SONA |
| **Memory-Augmented** | HNSW graph for O(log N) similarity search |
| **Multi-Model Routing** | FastGRNN routes to optimal models |
| **High Performance** | SIMD vectorization (AVX2/AVX512/NEON) |
| **Continual Learning** | LoRA adapters + EWC++ prevent forgetting |
| **Federated** | Distributed learning with privacy guarantees |
| **Edge-Ready** | WASM support for browser/edge deployment |

---

safla-complete_28.title: Overview
safla-complete_28.content:
SAFLA (Self-Aware Feedback Loop Algorithm) is a custom-built neural network designed specifically for AI agents and autonomous coding systems. It enhances AI agents and automated coding systems like Claude Code with:

- **Persistent Memory**: Never loses context across sessions
- **Self-Learning Capabilities**: Performance improves with each interaction
- **Adaptive Reasoning**: Learns what works best for different tasks
- **Built-in Safety**: Prevents harmful or dangerous operations
- **High Performance**: 172k+ operations/sec

safla-complete_38.title: Key Benefits
safla-complete_38.content:
| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Persistent Memory** | Remembers everything between sessions, smart recall, learns patterns |
| **Self-Learning** | Gets smarter over time, adapts strategies, continuous optimization |
| **Safe & Reliable** | Built-in safety constraints, rollback capability, real-time monitoring |
| **High Performance** | 172k+ ops/sec, real-time response, efficient memory compression |

safla-complete_47.title: Use Cases
safla-complete_47.content:
- **Claude Code** - MCP integration for autonomous development
- **Research Agents** - Persistent memory and knowledge building
- **Autonomous Systems** - Self-improving AI workflows
- **Enterprise AI** - Production-ready agent orchestration

---

safla-complete_100.title: System Architecture
safla-complete_100.content: SAFLA implements a layered architecture with clear separation of concerns, enabling scalable, maintainable, and safe autonomous AI operations.

safla-complete_104.title: Architectural Layers
safla-complete_104.content:
```
Application Layer
  ├── REST API
  ├── Command Line Interface
  ├── Python SDK
  └── Web Interface

Orchestration Layer
  ├── MCP Orchestrator
  ├── Agent Coordinator
  ├── Task Manager
  └── Workflow Engine

Cognitive Layer
  ├── Meta-Cognitive Engine
  ├── Goal Manager
  ├── Strategy Selector
  ├── Performance Monitor
  └── Adaptation Engine

Memory Layer
  ├── Vector Memory
  ├── Episodic Memory
  ├── Semantic Memory
  ├── Working Memory
  └── Memory Consolidation

Safety Layer
  ├── Safety Constraints
  ├── Validation Pipeline
  ├── Risk Assessment
  ├── Rollback Mechanisms
  └── Safety Monitoring

Infrastructure Layer
  ├── Storage Systems
  ├── Compute Resources
  ├── Network Services
  └── Security Services
```

safla-complete_147.title: Design Principles
safla-complete_147.content:
1. **Safety First** - All operations validated before execution, multiple safety layers
2. **Modularity** - Clear separation of concerns, well-defined interfaces
3. **Scalability** - Horizontal and vertical scaling capabilities
4. **Reliability** - Fault tolerance, error recovery, redundancy
5. **Maintainability** - Clean code, comprehensive logging, configuration-driven
6. **Security** - Multi-layer architecture, encryption, access control

safla-complete_156.title: Architectural Patterns
safla-complete_156.content:
- **Layered Architecture**: Separation of concerns with abstraction layers
- **Event-Driven Architecture**: Asynchronous processing, loose coupling
- **Microservices Pattern**: Service independence, technology diversity
- **Circuit Breaker Pattern**: Fault tolerance, quick recovery

---

safla-complete_165.title: Hybrid Memory System
safla-complete_165.content: SAFLA's hybrid memory architecture is inspired by human cognitive memory systems, implementing four specialized memory types that work together.

safla-complete_171.title: 1. Vector Memory System
safla-complete_171.content:
High-dimensional representations enabling similarity-based retrieval and semantic understanding.

**Features:**
- Multi-dimensional embeddings: 512, 768, 1024, 1536 dimensions
- Multiple similarity metrics: cosine, euclidean, dot product, manhattan
- Efficient indexing: HNSW, IVF, LSH, Flat Index
- Compression: Product Quantization, Scalar Quantization, Binary Quantization

**Performance Targets:**
- Vector search: <1ms for 10k vectors
- Batch operations: <10ms for 1000 vectors
- Memory efficiency: >80% storage optimization

```python
from safla.core.hybrid_memory import HybridMemoryArchitecture

memory = HybridMemoryArchitecture(
    vector_config={"embedding_dim": 768, "max_capacity": 10000},
    episodic_config={"max_capacity": 1000},
    working_config={"capacity": 10, "attention_window": 5}
)

safla-complete_195.title: Store and search
safla-complete_195.content:
memory_id = await memory.store_vector_memory(
    content="Machine learning is a subset of AI",
    embedding=embedding_vector,
    metadata={"type": "definition", "domain": "AI"}
)

similar_memories = await memory.search_similar_memories(
    query_embedding=query_vector,
    similarity_metric=SimilarityMetric.COSINE,
    top_k=10,
    similarity_threshold=0.8
)
```

safla-complete_210.title: 2. Episodic Memory System
safla-complete_210.content:
Sequential experiences with temporal context for learning from past interactions.

**Features:**
- Sequential experience storage with temporal ordering
- Context-outcome relationship tracking
- Event clustering and pattern recognition
- Causal relationship inference
- Experience replay for learning enhancement

```python
episode_id = await memory.store_episodic_memory(
    content="User requested explanation of neural networks",
    context={
        "user_id": "user_123",
        "session_id": "session_456",
        "previous_topics": ["machine_learning", "algorithms"]
    },
    outcome="provided_comprehensive_explanation",
    metadata={"duration": 180, "satisfaction_score": 0.9}
)

safla-complete_233.title: Temporal queries
safla-complete_233.content:
recent_episodes = await memory.get_episodes_by_timerange(
    start_time=datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=24),
    end_time=datetime.now()
)
```

safla-complete_240.title: 3. Semantic Memory System
safla-complete_240.content:
Knowledge graph for storing structured information and concept relationships.

**Features:**
- Graph-based knowledge representation
- Weighted relationship modeling
- Multi-hop inference capabilities
- Dynamic knowledge expansion
- Concept hierarchy management

**Graph Algorithms:**
- Traversal: BFS, DFS, Dijkstra's, PageRank
- Inference: Rule-based, Probabilistic, Analogical, Causal

```python

safla-complete_256.title: Add concept nodes

safla-complete_263.title: Create relationships
safla-complete_263.content:
await memory.add_semantic_edge(
    source_id=ml_node,
    target_id=ai_node,
    relationship="is_subset_of",
    weight=0.9
)

safla-complete_271.title: Query knowledge graph
safla-complete_271.content:
related_concepts = await memory.get_related_nodes(
    node_id=ai_node,
    relationship_types=["is_subset_of", "related_to"],
    max_depth=3
)
```

safla-complete_279.title: 4. Working Memory System
safla-complete_279.content:
Active context and immediate processing with attention mechanisms.

**Features:**
- Limited capacity (7 plus/minus 2 items, configurable)
- Attention-based focus management
- Temporal decay for automatic cleanup
- Integration with long-term memory
- Real-time context maintenance

```python
attention_config = {
    "capacity_limit": 7,
    "decay_rate": 0.1,
    "rehearsal_strength": 0.8,
    "priority_weights": {"relevance": 0.4, "recency": 0.3, "importance": 0.3}
}

await memory.working_memory.add_item(
    content="Current user query",
    priority=0.9,
    attention_weight=0.8
)

active_context = await memory.working_memory.get_active_context(max_items=5)
```

safla-complete_307.title: Memory Consolidation
safla-complete_307.content:
The consolidation engine manages transfer between memory systems based on importance, frequency, and relevance.

**Phases:**
1. Assessment: Importance scoring, access frequency, relevance analysis, novelty detection
2. Decision: Threshold evaluation, memory type routing, conflict resolution
3. Transfer: Information extraction, format transformation, integration, verification
4. Optimization: Data compression, index updates, cleanup operations

```python
await memory.consolidation_engine.consolidate_memories(
    source_memory="working",
    target_memories=["vector", "episodic", "semantic"],
    criteria={
        "min_importance": 0.8,
        "min_access_count": 3,
        "max_age": timedelta(hours=1)
    }
)
```

---

safla-complete_331.title: Meta-Cognitive Engine
safla-complete_331.content: The Meta-Cognitive Engine provides self-awareness and strategic reasoning, enabling autonomous learning, goal management, strategy selection, and adaptive behavior.

safla-complete_335.title: Components
safla-complete_335.content:
1. **Self-Awareness Layer**
   - Self-Model: Skills, limitations, resources, performance
   - State Monitoring: Current state, context, environment
   - Capability Assessment: Continuous skill evaluation
   - Performance Tracking: Metrics and trends

2. **Goal Management Layer**
   - Goal Hierarchy: Mission > Vision > Strategic > Operational > Task
   - Priority Management: Critical, High, Medium, Low
   - Goal Decomposition: Automatic breakdown into sub-goals
   - Progress Tracking: Success criteria evaluation

3. **Strategy Layer**
   - Strategy Repository: Learned, Template, Adaptive, Emergency strategies
   - Strategy Selection: Context matching, resource requirements, success probability
   - Plan Generation: Execution planning with resource allocation
   - Adaptation: Real-time strategy refinement

4. **Learning Layer**
   - Experience Capture: Action logging, outcome tracking
   - Pattern Recognition: Success/failure patterns, context patterns
   - Knowledge Extraction: Rule extraction, heuristic generation
   - Skill Development: Continuous capability enhancement

safla-complete_449.title: Meta-learning - improve the learning process itself

safla-complete_456.title: Safety & Validation Framework
safla-complete_456.content: SAFLA's safety framework implements comprehensive validation, risk assessment, and rollback capabilities for secure autonomous operation.

safla-complete_460.title: Multi-Layer Safety Architecture
safla-complete_460.content:
```
Input Safety Layer
  ├── Input Validation
  ├── Data Sanitization
  ├── Rate Limiting
  └── Authentication

Process Safety Layer
  ├── Safety Constraints
  ├── Real-time Monitoring
  ├── Circuit Breakers
  └── Timeout Controls

Output Safety Layer
  ├── Output Validation
  ├── Content Filtering
  ├── Approval Gates
  └── Audit Logging

System Safety Layer
  ├── Resource Limits
  ├── Emergency Stop
  ├── Rollback System
  └── Recovery Procedures
```

safla-complete_488.title: Safety Constraints Engine
safla-complete_488.content:
**Constraint Types:**
- **Hard Constraints**: Cannot be violated, emergency stop on breach
- **Soft Constraints**: Warnings and adjustments allowed
- **Dynamic Constraints**: Context-aware, adaptive limits

```python
from safla.core.safety_validation import SafetyConstraint, ConstraintType, ViolationAction

safla-complete_498.title: Hard constraint
safla-complete_498.content:
memory_constraint = SafetyConstraint(
    name="memory_limit",
    constraint_type=ConstraintType.HARD,
    rule="memory_usage <= 1000000000",  # 1GB
    threshold=1000000000,
    violation_action=ViolationAction.EMERGENCY_STOP,
    priority=1
)

safla-complete_508.title: Soft constraint
safla-complete_508.content:
performance_constraint = SafetyConstraint(
    name="response_time",
    constraint_type=ConstraintType.SOFT,
    rule="response_time <= 5.0",
    threshold=5.0,
    violation_action=ViolationAction.WARNING,
    priority=3
)

safla-complete_522.title: Validation Pipeline
safla-complete_522.content:
Multi-stage validation with comprehensive error handling:

1. **Pre-Processing Validation**: Input format, schema, range, type
2. **Security Validation**: Authentication, authorization, injection prevention
3. **Business Logic Validation**: Rules, consistency, dependencies, workflow
4. **Post-Processing Validation**: Output validation, quality, compliance

```python
from safla.core.safety_validation import ValidationPipeline, ValidationStage

pipeline = ValidationPipeline(
    timeout=30.0,
    fail_fast=False,
    parallel_execution=True
)

validation_result = await pipeline.execute(data=request_data, context=context)

if validation_result.is_valid:
    proceed_with_operation(validation_result.validated_data)
```

safla-complete_546.title: Risk Assessment Engine
safla-complete_546.content:
Quantifies potential risks using weighted factors:

```python
from safla.core.safety_validation import RiskFactor, RiskAssessment

def calculate_memory_risk(data):
    usage_ratio = data.get('memory_usage', 0) / data.get('memory_limit', 1000000000)
    if usage_ratio < 0.5: return 0.1
    elif usage_ratio < 0.7: return 0.3
    elif usage_ratio < 0.9: return 0.7
    else: return 0.95

memory_risk = RiskFactor(
    name="memory_risk",
    weight=0.3,
    calculator=calculate_memory_risk
)

risk_result = await risk_assessment.assess_risk(operation_data)
if risk_result.overall_score > 0.7:
    recommendations = await risk_assessment.get_mitigation_recommendations(risk_result)
```

safla-complete_571.title: Rollback System
safla-complete_571.content:
Safe reversion through automated checkpoint creation:

```python
from safla.core.safety_validation import CheckpointManager, CheckpointType

checkpoint_manager = CheckpointManager(
    storage_path="./checkpoints",
    max_checkpoints=50,
    compression_enabled=True,
    encryption_enabled=True
)

safla-complete_585.title: Create checkpoint
safla-complete_585.content:
checkpoint_id = await checkpoint_manager.create_checkpoint(
    name="before_major_update",
    checkpoint_type=CheckpointType.MANUAL,
    include_components=["memory_system", "meta_cognitive_engine"]
)

safla-complete_606.title: Emergency Stop System
safla-complete_606.content:
```python
from safla.core.safety_validation import EmergencyStopSystem, StopReason

emergency_stop = EmergencyStopSystem(
    response_time_ms=100,
    graceful_shutdown_timeout=30.0,
    force_shutdown_timeout=5.0
)

safla-complete_617.title: Register triggers

safla-complete_634.title: Delta Evaluation System
safla-complete_634.content: Formal framework for quantifying system improvements and measuring progress toward goals.

safla-complete_638.title: Delta Formula
safla-complete_638.content:
```
Delta_total = alpha_1 x Delta_performance + alpha_2 x Delta_efficiency + alpha_3 x Delta_stability + alpha_4 x Delta_capability
```

Where:
- **Delta_performance**: (current_reward - previous_reward) / tokens_used
- **Delta_efficiency**: (current_throughput - previous_throughput) / resource_used
- **Delta_stability**: 1 - divergence_score (with trend analysis)
- **Delta_capability**: new_capabilities / total_capabilities

safla-complete_650.title: Delta Categories
safla-complete_650.content:
| Category | Metrics |
|----------|---------|
| **Performance Deltas** | Speed, Accuracy, Efficiency, Throughput |
| **Quality Deltas** | Reliability, Consistency, Robustness, Maintainability |
| **Learning Deltas** | Knowledge, Skill, Adaptation, Insight |
| **Resource Deltas** | Memory, CPU, Storage, Network |

safla-complete_659.title: Implementation
safla-complete_659.content:
```python
from safla.core.delta_evaluation import DeltaEvaluator

evaluator = DeltaEvaluator()

result = evaluator.evaluate_delta(
    performance_data={
        'current_reward': 0.92,
        'previous_reward': 0.85,
        'tokens_used': 1000
    },
    efficiency_data={
        'current_throughput': 150,
        'previous_throughput': 120,
        'resource_used': 0.8
    },
    stability_data={'divergence_score': 0.15},
    capability_data={'new_capabilities': 2, 'total_capabilities': 10},
    context="performance_critical"
)

print(f"Total Delta: {result.total_delta}")
print(f"Improvement Detected: {result.is_improvement()}")
```

safla-complete_887.title: Agent Capabilities

safla-complete_893.title: 1. Autonomous Agent Pattern
safla-complete_893.content:
```python
class AutonomousAgent:
    def __init__(self):
        self.memory = HybridMemoryArchitecture()
        self.cognition = MetaCognitiveEngine()
        self.safety = SafetyValidationFramework()

    async def process_task(self, task):
        # Self-awareness check
        awareness = await self.cognition.assess_self_awareness()

        # Create goal
        goal = await self.cognition.create_goal_from_task(task)

        # Strategy selection
        strategy = await self.cognition.select_strategy(task.context)

        # Safety validation
        for action in strategy.actions:
            validation = await self.safety.validate_action(action)
            if validation.is_safe:
                result = await self.execute_action(action)
                await self.memory.store_experience(action, result)

        # Learning and adaptation
        await self.cognition.adapt_from_feedback(task.feedback)
```

safla-complete_923.title: 2. Collaborative Agent Pattern
safla-complete_923.content:
```python
class CollaborativeAgent:
    def __init__(self, agent_id):
        self.agent_id = agent_id
        self.mcp_client = FastMCPClient()
        self.shared_memory = HybridMemoryArchitecture()

    async def collaborate_on_task(self, task, other_agents):
        # Share context
        context_embedding = await self.encode_context(task)
        await self.shared_memory.store_shared_context(context_embedding)

        # Coordinate through MCP
        coordination_result = await self.mcp_client.call_tool(
            "agent_coordinator",
            {"task": task, "agents": other_agents}
        )

        # Execute and share results
        my_subtask = coordination_result.assignments[self.agent_id]
        result = await self.process_subtask(my_subtask)
        await self.shared_memory.store_collaborative_result(result)
```

safla-complete_949.title: 3. Learning Agent Pattern
safla-complete_949.content:
```python
class LearningAgent:
    def __init__(self):
        self.memory = HybridMemoryArchitecture()
        self.cognition = MetaCognitiveEngine()
        self.delta_evaluator = DeltaEvaluationSystem()

    async def continuous_learning_loop(self):
        while True:
            performance = await self.delta_evaluator.evaluate_current_state()
            opportunities = await self.cognition.identify_learning_gaps()

            for opportunity in opportunities:
                learning_action = await self.design_learning_experiment(opportunity)
                result = await self.execute_learning_action(learning_action)
                await self.memory.consolidate_learning(learning_action, result)
                await self.cognition.update_strategies(result)

            await asyncio.sleep(learning_interval)
```

synaptic-mesh-complete_27.title: Executive Summary
synaptic-mesh-complete_27.content: The **Synaptic Neural Mesh** is a revolutionary distributed neural fabric that fundamentally changes how AI systems coordinate and evolve. It creates a **peer-to-peer network of adaptive neural micro-networks** where intelligence emerges through distributed coordination rather than centralized processing.

synaptic-mesh-complete_31.title: Core Vision
synaptic-mesh-complete_31.content:
Each node in the mesh contains:
- **QuDAG**: Quantum-resistant DAG network for secure P2P messaging
- **ruv-FANN**: Fast neural networks compiled to WebAssembly with SIMD optimization
- **DAA**: Dynamic Agent Architecture for evolutionary behavior
- **Neural Mesh**: Distributed cognition coordination layer

synaptic-mesh-complete_39.title: Key Innovations
synaptic-mesh-complete_39.content:
1. **Ephemeral Intelligence**: Neural networks that exist only as needed
2. **Quantum-Resistant Security**: Post-quantum cryptography (ML-DSA, ML-KEM)
3. **WASM Neural Processing**: Universal deployment with SIMD acceleration
4. **Distributed Consensus**: QR-Avalanche algorithm for Byzantine fault tolerance
5. **Self-Evolving Topology**: Mesh adapts and optimizes autonomously

synaptic-mesh-complete_125.title: 1. QuDAG Core - Quantum-Resistant P2P Networking
synaptic-mesh-complete_125.content:
**Purpose**: Provides secure P2P messaging backbone with DAG-based consensus

**Features**:
- QR-Avalanche consensus algorithm
- ML-DSA and ML-KEM post-quantum cryptography
- Dark domain addressing system
- P2P discovery with Kademlia DHT
- ChaCha20-Poly1305 onion routing for anonymity

**Security Levels**:
- Level 1: ML-KEM-512 (128-bit security)
- Level 3: ML-KEM-768 (192-bit security) - Default
- Level 5: ML-KEM-1024 (256-bit security)

synaptic-mesh-complete_141.title: 2. ruv-FANN - Neural Networks with WASM
synaptic-mesh-complete_141.content:
**Purpose**: Lightweight neural network engine compiled to WebAssembly

**Features**:
- SIMD-accelerated matrix operations
- WebGPU support for GPU acceleration
- 27+ neural architectures (MLP, LSTM, CNN, Transformer)
- Memory-efficient model representation
- <100ms decision latency

**Supported Architectures**:
- MLP (Multi-Layer Perceptron) - Basic feedforward networks
- LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) - Sequence processing
- CNN (Convolutional Neural Networks) - Image/pattern recognition
- Transformer - Complex reasoning tasks
- Configurable custom architectures

synaptic-mesh-complete_159.title: 3. Neural Mesh - Distributed Cognition Layer
synaptic-mesh-complete_159.content:
**Purpose**: Coordinates distributed neural processing across the mesh

**Features**:
- Inter-network communication protocols
- Distributed learning algorithms
- Neural consensus mechanisms
- Global state synchronization
- Cross-agent learning protocols

synaptic-mesh-complete_170.title: 4. DAA Swarm - Dynamic Agent Architecture
synaptic-mesh-complete_170.content:
**Purpose**: Manages self-organizing agent behavior and evolution

**Features**:
- Self-organizing swarm behavior
- Economic resource allocation
- Fault tolerance and recovery
- Agent lifecycle management
- Byzantine fault tolerance

- `mesh_status` - Monitor mesh health
- `spike_monitor` - Real-time spike monitoring
- `mesh_train` - Train with patterns
- `pattern_inject` - Inject activation patterns
- `connectivity_analyze` - Analyze connectivity patterns
- `activity_heatmap` - Generate activity visualizations
- `prune_connections` - Remove weak connections
- `optimize_topology` - Optimize for efficiency
- `mesh_save` - Save mesh state
- `mesh_load` - Load saved mesh
- `assistant_connect` - Connect AI to mesh
- `thought_inject` - Inject AI thoughts
- `mesh_query` - Query mesh for insights
- `batch_neuron_create` - Create neurons in batch
- `batch_synapse_update` - Update synapses in batch
- `stream_activity` - Stream neural activity
- `stream_metrics` - Stream performance metrics

tier2-agents-catalog_42.title: Purpose
tier2-agents-catalog_42.content:
A powerful, extensible agent framework leveraging ReACT (Reasoning and Acting) methodology for autonomous task execution and human-in-the-loop collaboration. Designed as a foundational template for building sophisticated AI agents with structured reasoning capabilities.

**Problem Solved:** Provides a standardized, production-ready framework for creating AI agents that can reason about tasks, execute actions, and collaborate with humans when needed.

tier2-agents-catalog_48.title: Agent Architecture
tier2-agents-catalog_48.content:
```
hello_world_agent/
├── agent/
│   ├── main.py              # Entry point
│   ├── config/
│   │   ├── agents.yaml      # Agent definitions
│   │   └── tasks.yaml       # Task configurations
│   ├── tools/               # Custom tool implementations
│   └── docs/                # Documentation
├── .env                     # Environment configuration
└── requirements.txt         # Dependencies
```

**Core Architecture Components:**

1. **ReACT Methodology Integration**
   - Structured reasoning framework
   - Action-observation loops
   - Intelligent decision making
   - Self-correction capabilities

2. **CrewAI Task Management**
   - Multi-agent orchestration
   - Task delegation and coordination
   - Workflow management
   - Agent collaboration protocols

3. **OpenRouter API Integration**
   - Access to state-of-the-art LLMs
   - Model switching capabilities
   - Unified API interface
   - Cost optimization

tier2-agents-catalog_104.title: API/Interface
tier2-agents-catalog_104.content:
**Installation:**
```bash
pip install hello_agent
```

**CLI Interface:**
```bash
python agent/main.py
```

**Python API:**
```python
from hello_agent import Agent

agent = Agent(
    model="anthropic/claude-2",
    hitl_enabled=True
)

result = agent.execute("Research AI trends")
```

**REST API:**
- Endpoints available via FastAPI
- OpenAPI specification included
- Authentication via API keys

tier2-agents-catalog_148.title: Optional: HITL Settings
tier2-agents-catalog_148.content:
HITL_ENABLED=false
```

**Agent Configuration (agents.yaml):**
```yaml
researcher:
  role: Research Analyst
  goal: Conduct thorough research and analysis
  backstory: Expert researcher with deep analytical skills
  allow_delegation: true
  verbose: true
  tools:
    - search_tool
    - web_scraper

executor:
  role: Task Executor
  goal: Execute tasks with precision and efficiency
  backstory: Skilled implementer focused on quality
  allow_delegation: false
  verbose: true

analyzer:
  role: Quality Analyzer
  goal: Analyze outputs and provide recommendations
  backstory: Experienced analyst ensuring quality standards
  allow_delegation: true
  verbose: true
```

tier2-agents-catalog_179.title: Examples
tier2-agents-catalog_179.content:
**Basic Research Task:**
```python
from hello_agent import Agent, Task

agent = Agent()
task = Task(
    description="Research the latest AI developments",
    expected_output="Comprehensive report",
    agent=agent.researcher
)
result = agent.execute(task)
```

**Multi-Agent Workflow:**
```python
from hello_agent import Crew

crew = Crew(
    agents=[researcher, executor, analyzer],
    tasks=[research_task, execute_task, analyze_task],
    process="sequential"
)
result = crew.kickoff()
```

**HITL Integration:**
```python
agent = Agent(hitl_enabled=True)

tier2-agents-catalog_210.title: Agent will pause for human validation at critical points
tier2-agents-catalog_210.content:
result = agent.execute(
    task="Deploy production changes",
    require_approval=True
)
```

tier2-agents-catalog_334.title: Configuration
tier2-agents-catalog_334.content:
**Environment Variables (.env):**
```env
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
SLACK_WEBHOOK=https://hooks.slack.com/...
```

**Configuration File (config.yml):**
```yaml
scanning:
  enabled: true
  tools:
    - zap
    - nuclei
    - dependency-check

  zap:
    target_url: "http://localhost:8080"
    scan_type: "full"

  nuclei:
    templates:
      - cves
      - vulnerabilities
      - misconfigurations

analysis:
  ai_model: "gpt-4"
  severity_threshold: "medium"
  auto_fix: true

remediation:
  model: "claude-3-sonnet"
  create_branch: true
  create_pr: true

notifications:

tier2-agents-catalog_554.title: Purpose
tier2-agents-catalog_554.content:
A secure registry for AI agents based on the OWASP GenAI Security Project's Agent Name Service (ANS) Protocol. Provides a foundational framework for secure discovery and interaction among AI agents.

**Problem Solved:** Enables standardized agent registration, discovery, and secure communication across heterogeneous AI systems using industry-standard protocols (A2A and MCP).

tier2-agents-catalog_560.title: Agent Architecture
tier2-agents-catalog_560.content:
```
agent-name-service/
├── src/
│   ├── ans.ts              # Core ANS service
│   ├── certificate.ts      # Certificate management
│   ├── db.ts               # SQLite persistence
│   ├── mastra.ts           # Threat modeling (full)
│   ├── mastra-simple.ts    # Threat modeling (lite)
│   ├── protocols.ts        # A2A/MCP formatters
│   └── types/              # TypeScript definitions
├── examples/
│   ├── usage.ts            # Complete example
│   └── basic_registration.ts
├── tests/                  # Test suites
└── docs/                   # Documentation
```

**ANS Architecture:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   Agent Name Service                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐         │
│  │ Certificate │  │   Agent     │  │   Threat    │         │
│  │   Service   │  │  Registry   │  │  Analysis   │         │
│  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ┌─────────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐          │
│  │    A2A Protocol     │  │    MCP Protocol     │          │
│  │     Formatter       │  │     Formatter       │          │
│  └─────────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

tier2-agents-catalog_596.title: Capabilities
tier2-agents-catalog_596.content:
| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Secure Registration | Agents registered with unique names and certificates |
| Certificate Management | X.509 certificate generation and validation |
| Multi-Protocol Support | A2A and MCP format resolution |
| Threat Analysis | Mastra.ai integration for security assessment |
| SQLite Persistence | Lightweight storage for easy deployment |
| Modular Architecture | Clean separation for extensibility |

tier2-agents-catalog_821.title: Examples
tier2-agents-catalog_821.content:
**Agent Creation:**
```python
import requests

agent = {
    "name": "HR Assistant",
    "type": "hr",
    "capabilities": ["recruiting", "onboarding"],
    "llm_config": {
        "model": "gpt-4",
        "temperature": 0.7
    }
}

response = requests.post(
    "http://localhost:8000/api/agents",
    json=agent
)
```

**Task Assignment:**
```python
task = {
    "title": "Screen candidates",
    "description": "Review 50 applications",
    "priority": "high",
    "agent_id": "hr-001"
}

response = requests.post(
    "http://localhost:8000/api/tasks",
    json=task
)
```

tier2-agents-catalog_1703.title: API/Interface
tier2-agents-catalog_1703.content:
**Provider Registration:**
```python
from capacity_exchange import Provider

provider = Provider(
    gpus=[
        {"type": "A100", "count": 4, "memory": 80},
        {"type": "H100", "count": 2, "memory": 80}
    ],
    pricing={
        "A100": 2.50,  # per GPU hour
        "H100": 4.00
    }
)

await provider.register()
```

**Consumer Request:**
```python
from capacity_exchange import Consumer

consumer = Consumer()

allocation = await consumer.request(
    gpu_type="A100",
    count=2,
    duration_hours=4,
    max_price=3.00
)

tier2-agents-catalog_1823.title: Common Architectural Patterns
tier2-agents-catalog_1823.content:
1. **ReACT Methodology**
   - Used by: hello_world_agent, agentic-security, agileagents
   - Pattern: Reasoning -> Action -> Observation loop

2. **Multi-Model Pipelines**
   - Used by: agentic-security, agentic-reports, agentXNG
   - Pattern: Chain different LLMs for specialized tasks

3. **FastAPI Backend**
   - Used by: agentic-employment, agentic-scraper, agentic-preview, agentic-capacity-exchange
   - Pattern: RESTful API with async handlers

4. **Gradio Frontend**
   - Used by: agentic-employment, agentic-gradio, agentic-dashboard
   - Pattern: Rapid UI prototyping

5. **Firecracker Isolation**
   - Used by: agentic-capacity-exchange
   - Pattern: MicroVM-based workload isolation

tier2-agents-catalog_2068.title: Agent class
tier2-agents-catalog_2068.content:
class Agent:
    """Core agent with ReACT methodology."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        model: str = "anthropic/claude-2",
        hitl_enabled: bool = False,
        verbose: bool = True,
        tools: List[Tool] = None
    ):
        """Initialize agent with configuration."""

    async def execute(
        self,
        task: Union[str, Task],
        require_approval: bool = False,
        timeout: int = 300
    ) -> ExecutionResult:
        """Execute a task using ReACT methodology."""

    async def research(
        self,
        topic: str,
        depth: int = 3,
        sources: List[str] = None
    ) -> ResearchResult:
        """Conduct research on a topic."""

    async def analyze(
        self,
        data: Any,
        analysis_type: str = "comprehensive"
    ) -> AnalysisResult:
        """Analyze data with specified analysis type."""

tier2-agents-catalog_2363.title: Exchange class
tier2-agents-catalog_2363.content:
class CapacityExchange:
    """Decentralized GPU capacity marketplace."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        config: ExchangeConfig = None
    ):
        """Initialize exchange."""

    async def register_provider(
        self,
        provider: Provider
    ) -> RegistrationResult:
        """Register GPU provider."""

    async def submit_job(
        self,
        job: Job
    ) -> JobSubmission:
        """Submit compute job."""

    async def get_pricing(
        self,
        requirements: ComputeRequirements
    ) -> PricingEstimate:
        """Get pricing for compute requirements."""

    async def get_availability(
        self,
        gpu_type: str = None,
        location: str = None
    ) -> List[AvailableCapacity]:
        """Check available capacity."""

tier2-agents-catalog_2398.title: Provider class

class Provider:

        self,
        name: str,

        location: str = None
    ):

        """Register with exchange."""

    async def update_availability(
        self,

tier2-agents-catalog_2531.title: agents.yaml - Full agent configuration
tier2-agents-catalog_2531.content:
researcher:
  role: Senior Research Analyst
  goal: >
    Conduct comprehensive research and analysis on assigned topics,
    synthesizing information from multiple sources into actionable insights.
  backstory: >
    You are an expert researcher with decades of experience in
    information gathering, analysis, and synthesis. You excel at
    finding relevant information, identifying patterns, and
    presenting findings in a clear, structured format.
  allow_delegation: true
  verbose: true
  max_iter: 15
  max_rpm: 100
  llm: anthropic/claude-2
  tools:
    - search_tool
    - web_scraper
    - document_analyzer
    - citation_manager

executor:
  role: Task Execution Specialist
  goal: >
    Execute tasks with precision, efficiency, and attention to detail,
    ensuring high-quality outputs that meet specifications.
  backstory: >
    You are a meticulous executor known for delivering exceptional
    results. You approach each task systematically, validating
    requirements and producing outputs that exceed expectations.
  allow_delegation: false
  verbose: true
  max_iter: 10
  llm: anthropic/claude-2
  tools:
    - code_executor
    - file_manager
    - api_client

analyzer:
  role: Quality Assurance Analyst
  goal: >
    Analyze outputs for quality, accuracy, and completeness,
    providing detailed feedback and improvement recommendations.
  backstory: >
    You are a seasoned analyst with expertise in quality assurance.
    Your keen eye for detail and systematic approach ensures
    outputs meet the highest standards.
  allow_delegation: true
  verbose: true
  max_iter: 8
  llm: anthropic/claude-2
  tools:
    - quality_checker
    - metrics_calculator
    - report_generator

tier2-agents-catalog_2589.title: tasks.yaml - Task definitions
tier2-agents-catalog_2589.content:
research_task:
  description: >
    Research {topic} comprehensively, gathering information from
    authoritative sources. Analyze the current state, identify
    trends, and synthesize findings into a structured report.
  expected_output: >
    A comprehensive research report with:
    - Executive summary
    - Current state analysis
    - Key trends and patterns
    - Supporting evidence
    - Recommendations
  agent: researcher
  async_execution: false
  human_input: false

execution_task:
  description: >
    Based on the research findings, execute {action} with
    attention to quality and completeness.
  expected_output: >
    Completed deliverable that:
    - Meets all specifications
    - Incorporates research insights
    - Is production-ready
  agent: executor
  context:
    - research_task
  async_execution: false

analysis_task:
  description: >
    Analyze the execution output for quality, accuracy, and
    completeness. Provide detailed feedback and recommendations.
  expected_output: >
    Quality analysis report with:
    - Quality score
    - Detailed findings
    - Improvement recommendations
    - Final assessment
  agent: analyzer
  context:
    - execution_task
  async_execution: false
  human_input: true
```

general:
  project_name: "my-project"
  version: "1.0.0"
  environment: "development"

tier2-agents-catalog_2650.title: Scanning configuration
tier2-agents-catalog_2650.content:
scanning:
  enabled: true
  parallel: true
  max_workers: 4

  # OWASP ZAP configuration
  zap:
    enabled: true
    target_url: "${TARGET_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"
    api_key: "${ZAP_API_KEY}"
    scan_type: "full"  # passive, active, full
    ajax_spider: true
    max_duration: 3600
    policies:
      - name: "Default Policy"
        strength: "Medium"
        threshold: "Medium"
    excluded_urls:
      - ".*logout.*"
      - ".*static.*"

  # Nuclei configuration
  nuclei:
    enabled: true
    templates:
      - cves
      - vulnerabilities
      - misconfigurations
      - exposures
      - technologies
      - workflows
    severity:
      - critical
      - high
      - medium
    rate_limit: 150
    bulk_size: 25
    concurrency: 25
    retries: 2

  # Dependency check configuration
  dependency_check:
    enabled: true
    fail_on_cvss: 7.0
    suppression_file: "./dependency-check-suppression.xml"
    analyzers:
      - jar

tier2-agents-catalog_2955.title: Agent Development Best Practices
tier2-agents-catalog_2955.content:
1. **Design for Composability**
   - Create single-purpose agents
   - Use clear interfaces
   - Enable agent chaining

2. **Implement Robust Error Handling**
   - Use retry logic with exponential backoff
   - Implement circuit breakers
   - Log all errors with context

3. **Optimize for Performance**
   - Cache expensive operations
   - Use async/await properly
   - Implement connection pooling

4. **Ensure Security**
   - Never hardcode credentials
   - Validate all inputs
   - Use least-privilege access

5. **Enable Observability**
   - Implement structured logging
   - Add metrics collection
   - Enable distributed tracing

tier2-agents-catalog_2982.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration Best Practices
tier2-agents-catalog_2982.content:
1. **Define Clear Roles**
   - One agent per responsibility
   - Document agent capabilities
   - Avoid role overlap

2. **Manage Dependencies**
   - Use explicit task dependencies
   - Handle circular dependencies
   - Implement timeout handling

3. **Coordinate State**
   - Use shared memory for coordination
   - Implement state synchronization
   - Handle race conditions

4. **Scale Appropriately**
   - Start with minimal agents
   - Add agents based on bottlenecks
   - Monitor resource usage

tier2-agents-catalog_3006.title: Conclusion
tier2-agents-catalog_3006.content:
This catalog documents 23 specialized AI agent repositories from the RuvNet ecosystem. Each agent addresses specific domain needs:

- **Development Assistance**: hello_world_agent, agentXNG, agentX, micro-agent
- **Security**: agentic-security, Agent-Name-Service
- **Communication**: agentic-voice, phone-agent
- **Operations**: agentic-devops, agentic-preview, agentic-employment
- **Data Processing**: agentic-reports, agentic-scraper, agentic-search
- **Creative**: agentic-difusion, agentic-music, agentic-artifacts
- **Infrastructure**: agentic-capacity-exchange, agentic-dashboard
- **Workflow**: agileagents, agentic-flows, agentic-gradio
- **Robotics**: agentic-robotics

The agents share common patterns including ReACT methodology, FastAPI backends, Gradio frontends, and multi-model LLM pipelines. Integration is facilitated through the Agent Name Service (ANS) protocol supporting both A2A and MCP formats.

**Key Integration Points:**
- All agents can register with ANS for discovery
- MCP manifests enable standardized tool exposure
- A2A cards enable agent-to-agent communication
- Shared memory enables state coordination

**Recommended Learning Path:**
1. Start with `hello_world_agent` for fundamentals
2. Explore `agentic-security` for security patterns
3. Study `Agent-Name-Service` for multi-agent registration
4. Implement `agentic-capacity-exchange` for distributed computing

---

tier3-specialized-systems_34.title: Specialized Purpose
tier3-specialized-systems_34.content: WiFi DensePose is a cutting-edge privacy-preserving human pose estimation system that uses WiFi Channel State Information (CSI) instead of cameras. It provides real-time pose detection without visual surveillance.

tier3-specialized-systems_38.title: Primary Use Cases
tier3-specialized-systems_38.content:
1. **Healthcare Monitoring**: Patient pose tracking in hospitals without privacy concerns
2. **Smart Home Automation**: Activity recognition for elderly care and home automation
3. **Fitness Applications**: Exercise form tracking without camera installation
4. **Security Systems**: Occupancy monitoring and fall detection
5. **Privacy-Sensitive Environments**: Pose estimation where cameras are prohibited

tier3-specialized-systems_84.content:

|-----------|----------|------------|
| CSI Processor | Extract Channel State Information from WiFi | Signal processing |
| Phase Sanitizer | Remove hardware phase offsets and noise | Phase correction algorithms |
| DensePose Neural Network | Convert CSI to human pose keypoints | Deep learning |
| Multi-Person Tracker | Maintain person identities across frames | MOT algorithms |
| Analytics Engine | Fall detection, activity recognition | Pattern recognition |

tier3-specialized-systems_105.title: Phase sanitization removes random phase offsets
tier3-specialized-systems_105.content:
def sanitize_phase(csi_matrix):
    # Linear phase calibration
    phase_offset = compute_phase_offset(csi_matrix)
    sanitized = csi_matrix * np.exp(-1j * phase_offset)
    return sanitized
```

async def smart_home_automation():

tier3-specialized-systems_491.title: Specialized Purpose
tier3-specialized-systems_491.content: FACT revolutionizes LLM data retrieval by replacing traditional RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) with intelligent prompt caching and deterministic MCP tool execution. It delivers sub-100ms responses with 60-90% cost reduction.

tier3-specialized-systems_495.title: Why FACT Replaces RAG
tier3-specialized-systems_495.content:
| Aspect | Traditional RAG | FACT |
|--------|----------------|------|
| Retrieval Mechanism | Embeddings -> Vector search -> LLM | Prompt cache -> MCP tools -> LLM |
| Data Freshness | Periodic re-indexing | Live data via on-demand tools |
| Accuracy | Probabilistic, fuzzy matches | Exact outputs from SQL, API, tools |
| Cost & Latency | Embedding + lookup + tokens | Cache hits = 0 tokens |

tier3-specialized-systems_504.title: Technical Architecture
tier3-specialized-systems_504.content:
```
Traditional RAG:
User Query -> Embedding -> Vector Search -> Context -> LLM -> Response (2-5s)

FACT Approach:
User Query -> Prompt Cache -> [If Miss] -> MCP Tool -> Cache Update -> Response (50-200ms)
```

tier3-specialized-systems_514.title: Intelligent Cache Strategy Engine
tier3-specialized-systems_514.content:
```
Cache Strategy Engine:
+-- Static Content -> Long-term cache (hours/days)
|   +-- System prompts and schemas
|   +-- Configuration data
|   +-- Reference documentation
|
+-- Semi-Dynamic -> Medium-term cache (minutes/hours)
|   +-- Database schemas
|   +-- User preferences
|   +-- System metrics
|
+-- Dynamic Content -> Short-term cache (seconds/minutes)
    +-- Live API responses
    +-- Real-time calculations
    +-- User-specific queries
```

tier3-specialized-systems_536.title: 1. Agentic Multi-Step Query Processing
tier3-specialized-systems_536.content:
```
Complex Query: "Generate Q1 sales report with trend analysis and recommendations"

Step 1: Cache Check -> System prompts (CACHE HIT - 0ms)
Step 2: Tool Execution -> Fetch Q1 sales data (Database query - 45ms)
Step 3: Cache Decision -> Store raw data (TTL: 1 hour)
Step 4: Tool Execution -> Calculate trends (Analysis tool - 23ms)
Step 5: Cache Decision -> Store trends (TTL: 30 min)
Step 6: Tool Execution -> Generate recommendations (AI reasoning - 67ms)
Step 7: Cache Decision -> Short TTL (5 min - context-specific)
Step 8: Response Assembly -> Final report (8ms)

Total Time: 143ms (vs. 3+ seconds with RAG)
```

import asyncio
from src.core.driver import get_driver

async def financial_analysis():

    result = await driver.process_query(
        query="What are quarterly revenue trends for tech companies?",
        include_metadata=True,
        cache_strategy="intelligent"
    )

    print(f"Response: {result.response}")
    print(f"Performance: {result.response_time_ms}ms")
    print(f"Cache Status: {'HIT' if result.cache_hit else 'MISS'}")
    print(f"Cost: ${result.cost:.4f}")
    print(f"Tools Used: {', '.join(result.tools_used)}")

tier3-specialized-systems_1005.title: 2. Algorithm Selection Hierarchy
tier3-specialized-systems_1005.content:
| Method | Complexity | Best For |
|--------|------------|----------|
| solveTrueSublinear | O(log n) | Diagonally dominant, needs TRUE sublinear |
| neumann | O(k*nnz) | Diagonally dominant matrices |
| forward-push | O(1/epsilon) | Sparse systems with local structure |
| backward-push | O(1/epsilon) | Systems with known target nodes |
| random-walk | O(sqrt(n)/epsilon) | Large sparse graphs |
| auto | Best available | Automatic optimization |

tier3-specialized-systems_1389.title: Specialized Purpose
tier3-specialized-systems_1389.content: Ultrasonic Agentics is a steganography framework for embedding encrypted AI commands into ultrasonic frequencies (18-20 kHz) - inaudible to humans but detectable by applications. Perfect for covert communication and AI agent coordination.

tier3-specialized-systems_1393.title: Key Use Cases
tier3-specialized-systems_1393.content:
- **AI Agent Coordination**: Covert command transmission between AI systems
- **Low-Power Command & Control**: Battery-powered IoT sensors
- **Emergency Communications**: Backup channel when networks fail
- **Digital Watermarking**: Invisible content signatures
- **Smart Home Automation**: Inaudible device control
- **Underwater Communications**: Submarine data transmission

tier3-specialized-systems_1402.title: Technical Architecture
tier3-specialized-systems_1402.content:
```
1. Command Encryption -> AES-256-GCM with key derivation
2. Binary Encoding -> Efficient bit packing with error correction
3. FSK Modulation -> Frequency-shift keying at 18.5-19.5 kHz
4. Signal Injection -> Psychoacoustic masking for seamless integration
5. Adaptive Decoding -> ML-enhanced signal detection and extraction
```

tier3-specialized-systems_1412.title: Security Features
tier3-specialized-systems_1412.content:
- **End-to-End Encryption**: Commands never transmitted in plaintext
- **Authentication**: HMAC prevents tampering
- **Key Management**: Secure key generation and rotation
- **Obfuscation**: Multiple encoding layers prevent detection

tier3-specialized-systems_1425.title: CLI Usage
tier3-specialized-systems_1425.content:
ultrasonic-agentics embed -i input.mp3 -o output.mp3 -c "command:execute" -k secret-key
ultrasonic-agentics decode -i output.mp3 -k secret-key
ultrasonic-agentics analyze -i audio.mp3 --spectrogram

tier3-specialized-systems_1442.title: Embed a command
tier3-specialized-systems_1442.content:
embedder = AudioEmbedder()
command = "execute: deploy_model --version 2.0"
secure_audio = embedder.embed_from_file("original.mp3", command)
secure_audio.export("output.mp3", format="mp3")

tier3-specialized-systems_1448.title: Decode the command
tier3-specialized-systems_1448.content:
decoder = AudioDecoder(embedder.cipher.key)
decoded_command = decoder.decode_from_file("output.mp3")
print(f"Hidden command: {decoded_command}")
```

tier3-specialized-systems_1520.title: Creating Projects
tier3-specialized-systems_1520.content:
```python
import asyncio
from fastmcp import FastMCPClient

async def create_project():
    client = FastMCPClient("http://localhost:3000")

    # Create a Python package project
    project_path = await client.call_tool("create_project", {
        "template_name": "python-package",
        "output_dir": "projects",
        "variables": {
            "project_name": "My Project",
            "project_slug": "my_project",
            "full_name": "John Doe",
            "email": "john@example.com",
            "use_pytest": "y",
            "use_black": "y"
        }
    })

    print(f"Created at: {project_path}")

asyncio.run(create_project())
```

tier3-specialized-systems_1548.title: Database Schema
tier3-specialized-systems_1548.content:
```sql
-- templates table
CREATE TABLE templates (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    url TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    category TEXT,
    tags TEXT,  -- Comma-separated
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- template_versions table
CREATE TABLE template_versions (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    template_id INTEGER REFERENCES templates(id),
    version TEXT,
    git_hash TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
```

tier3-specialized-systems_2034.title: Summary
tier3-specialized-systems_2034.content:
Tier 3 of the RuvNet ecosystem provides 14 specialized systems that extend core capabilities into advanced domains:

1. **Computer Vision**: wifi-densepose (privacy-preserving pose estimation)
2. **Neural Networks**: ruv-FANN (ephemeral intelligence framework)
3. **Data Retrieval**: FACT (replaces RAG with intelligent caching)
4. **Cryptography**: QuDAG (quantum-resistant distributed systems)
5. **Distributed AI**: federated-mcp (cross-organization AI federation)
6. **Mathematics**: sublinear-time-solver (TRUE O(log n) algorithms)
7. **Real-time AI**: midstream (LLM streaming with temporal analysis)
8. **AI Safety**: guardrail (ethical AI with emotional intelligence)
9. **Programming**: promptlang (AI-native programming language)
10. **Steganography**: ultrasonic (covert audio communication)

tier4-integrations_209.title: Integration Examples
tier4-integrations_209.content:
**Conversion Script:**
```python
import json
import yaml

def convert_swagger_to_plugin(swagger_url):
    """Convert Swagger spec to ChatGPT plugin format."""
    swagger = fetch_swagger(swagger_url)

    # Generate ai-plugin.json
    plugin_manifest = {
        "schema_version": "v1",
        "name_for_human": swagger["info"]["title"],
        "name_for_model": slugify(swagger["info"]["title"]),
        "description_for_model": generate_model_description(swagger),
        "api": {
            "type": "openapi",
            "url": "/openapi.yaml"
        }
    }

    # Convert Swagger 2.0 to OpenAPI 3.0
    openapi_spec = convert_to_openapi3(swagger)

    return plugin_manifest, openapi_spec

def generate_model_description(swagger):
    """Generate optimized description for ChatGPT model."""
    endpoints = []
    for path, methods in swagger["paths"].items():
        for method, details in methods.items():
            endpoints.append(f"{method.upper()} {path}: {details.get('summary', '')}")

    return f"API with endpoints: {'; '.join(endpoints)}"
```

**Key Features:**
- Swagger 2.0 to OpenAPI 3.0 conversion
- Automatic manifest generation
- Model-optimized descriptions
- Authentication configuration
- Endpoint mapping

tier4-integrations_429.title: Integration Examples
tier4-integrations_429.content:
**Core Summarization Logic:**
```python
from fastapi import FastAPI
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import openai
import httpx
import asyncio

app = FastAPI()

async def fetch_url_content(url: str) -> str:
    """Fetch HTML content from URL."""
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        response = await client.get(url)
        return response.text

def extract_text(html: str) -> str:
    """Extract text content from HTML."""
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, 'html.parser')

    # Remove script and style elements
    for script in soup(["script", "style"]):
        script.decompose()

    return soup.get_text(separator='\n', strip=True)

async def summarize_chunk(chunk: str) -> str:
    """Summarize a text chunk using GPT-3.5."""
    response = await openai.ChatCompletion.acreate(
        model="gpt-3.5-turbo",
        messages=[
            {"role": "system", "content": "Summarize the following text in bullet points."},
            {"role": "user", "content": chunk}
        ],
        max_tokens=500
    )
    return response.choices[0].message.content

async def summarize_article(text: str) -> str:
    """Summarize article using concurrent API calls."""
    # Split into chunks
    chunks = [text[i:i+3000] for i in range(0, len(text), 3000)]

    # Process chunks concurrently
    tasks = [summarize_chunk(chunk) for chunk in chunks]
    summaries = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)

    # Combine summaries
    combined = "\n".join(summaries)

    # Final summary
    return await summarize_chunk(combined)

@app.post("/summarize")
async def summarize_url(request: dict):
    """Main endpoint for URL summarization."""
    url = request.get("url")

    # Fetch and process
    html = await fetch_url_content(url)
    text = extract_text(html)
    summary = await summarize_article(text)

    return {"summary": summary, "url": url}
```

**Concurrent Processing:**
```python
import asyncio
from typing import List

async def process_multiple_urls(urls: List[str]) -> List[dict]:

tier4-integrations_508.title: Usage
tier4-integrations_508.content:
urls = [
    "https://example.com/article1",
    "https://example.com/article2",
    "https://example.com/article3"
]
summaries = asyncio.run(process_multiple_urls(urls))
```

**Key Features:**
- Web content fetching and parsing
- BeautifulSoup HTML extraction
- Concurrent GPT-3.5 API calls
- Token count management
- Chunk-based processing
- FastAPI backend
- Jinja2 templates for web UI

tier4-integrations_1122.title: 8. q-star
tier4-integrations_1122.content:
**Integration Type:** Q-Learning + Microsoft AutoGen Agents

**Overview:**
Implementation of Q* (Q-Star) reinforcement learning algorithm integrated with Microsoft AutoGen for multi-agent systems. Combines Q-learning optimization with autonomous agent collaboration.

tier4-integrations_1163.title: API Structure
tier4-integrations_1163.content:
**Q-Learning Agent:**
```python
import numpy as np
from typing import Dict, Any

class QLearningAgent:
    def __init__(
        self,
        state_size: int,
        action_size: int,
        learning_rate: float = 0.1,
        discount_factor: float = 0.99,
        exploration_rate: float = 1.0,
        exploration_decay: float = 0.995,
        min_exploration: float = 0.01
    ):
        self.state_size = state_size
        self.action_size = action_size
        self.learning_rate = learning_rate
        self.discount_factor = discount_factor
        self.exploration_rate = exploration_rate
        self.exploration_decay = exploration_decay
        self.min_exploration = min_exploration

        # Initialize Q-table
        self.q_table = np.zeros((state_size, action_size))

    def choose_action(self, state: int) -> int:
        """Epsilon-greedy action selection."""
        if np.random.random() < self.exploration_rate:
            return np.random.randint(self.action_size)
        return np.argmax(self.q_table[state])

    def update(
        self,
        state: int,
        action: int,
        reward: float,
        next_state: int,
        done: bool
    ):
        """Update Q-value using Bellman equation."""
        current_q = self.q_table[state, action]

        if done:
            target_q = reward
        else:
            target_q = reward + self.discount_factor * np.max(self.q_table[next_state])

        # Q-learning update
        self.q_table[state, action] = current_q + self.learning_rate * (target_q - current_q)

        # Decay exploration
        self.exploration_rate = max(
            self.min_exploration,
            self.exploration_rate * self.exploration_decay
        )
```

**Q* Algorithm Implementation:**
```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

class QStarNetwork(nn.Module):
    """Deep Q-Network with A* heuristic guidance."""

    def __init__(self, state_dim: int, action_dim: int, hidden_dim: int = 256):
        super().__init__()

        self.feature_extractor = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(state_dim, hidden_dim),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, hidden_dim),
            nn.ReLU()
        )

        # Q-value head
        self.q_head = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, action_dim)

        # Heuristic head (A* guidance)
        self.heuristic_head = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, action_dim)

    def forward(self, state: torch.Tensor) -> tuple:
        features = self.feature_extractor(state)
        q_values = self.q_head(features)
        heuristic = self.heuristic_head(features)

        # Q* combines Q-values with heuristic guidance
        q_star = q_values + heuristic

        return q_star, q_values, heuristic

class QStarAgent:
    def __init__(self, state_dim: int, action_dim: int):
        self.network = QStarNetwork(state_dim, action_dim)
        self.optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(self.network.parameters(), lr=1e-4)
        self.memory = []

    def train_step(self, batch):
        states, actions, rewards, next_states, dones = batch

        q_star, q_values, _ = self.network(states)
        next_q_star, _, _ = self.network(next_states)

        # Q* target
        target = rewards + 0.99 * next_q_star.max(1)[0] * (1 - dones)

        # Loss
        current_q = q_values.gather(1, actions.unsqueeze(1))
        loss = nn.MSELoss()(current_q.squeeze(), target)

        self.optimizer.zero_grad()
        loss.backward()
        self.optimizer.step()

        return loss.item()
```

**Key Features:**
- Q-Learning implementation
- Microsoft AutoGen integration
- Multi-agent co

tier4-integrations_1356.title: 9. reflective-engineer
tier4-integrations_1356.content:
**Integration Type:** LangChain Agent Development Environment

**Overview:**
Advanced LangChain-based agent development toolkit featuring reflection capabilities, multiple reasoning strategies (Chain of Thought, Tree of Thoughts), and sophisticated memory systems.

tier4-integrations_1383.title: API Structure
tier4-integrations_1383.content:
**Reflective Agent:**
```python
from langchain.chat_models import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_openai_tools_agent
from langchain.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate, MessagesPlaceholder
from langchain.memory import ConversationBufferWindowMemory

class ReflectiveAgent:
    def __init__(self, model_name: str = "gpt-4"):
        self.llm = ChatOpenAI(model=model_name, temperature=0.7)
        self.memory = ConversationBufferWindowMemory(
            k=10,
            memory_key="chat_history",
            return_messages=True
        )

        self.prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
            ("system", """You are a reflective AI assistant that thinks step by step.

            For each response:
            1. Analyze the problem thoroughly
            2. Consider multiple approaches
            3. Reflect on potential issues
            4. Provide a well-reasoned answer

            Use <thinking> tags for internal reasoning.
            Use <reflection> tags to critique your own thinking.
            Use <output> tags for the final response."""),
            MessagesPlaceholder(variable_name="chat_history"),
            ("human", "{input}"),
            MessagesPlaceholder(variable_name="agent_scratchpad")
        ])

    def reflect(self, thought: str) -> str:
        """Generate reflection on a thought."""
        reflection_prompt = f"""Reflect on this reasoning:

        {thought}

        Consider:
        - Are there logical gaps?
        - What assumptions were made?
        - Are there alternative perspectives?
        - What could be improved?"""

        return self.llm.predict(reflection_prompt)
```

tier4-integrations_1433.title: Integration Examples
tier4-integrations_1433.content:
**Chain of Thought Reasoning:**
```python
from langchain.prompts import PromptTemplate
from langchain.chains import LLMChain

cot_template = """Solve this problem step by step:

Problem: {problem}

Let me think through this carefully:

Step 1: Understand the problem
{step1_reasoning}

Step 2: Break down into components
{step2_reasoning}

Step 3: Solve each component
{step3_reasoning}

Step 4: Combine and verify
{step4_reasoning}

Final Answer: {final_answer}
"""

class ChainOfThoughtAgent:
    def __init__(self):
        self.llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4", temperature=0)

    def solve(self, problem: str) -> dict:
        steps = []

        # Step 1: Understand
        understand_prompt = f"Understand this problem and identify key elements: {problem}"
        step1 = self.llm.predict(understand_prompt)
        steps.append(("Understanding", step1))

        # Step 2: Break down
        breakdown_prompt = f"Given: {step1}\nBreak down into sub-problems:"
        step2 = self.llm.predict(breakdown_prompt)
        steps.append(("Breakdown", step2))

        # Step 3: Solve components
        solve_prompt = f"Given breakdown: {step2}\nSolve each component:"
        step3 = self.llm.predict(solve_prompt)
        steps.append(("Solution", step3))

        # Step 4: Verify
        verify_prompt = f"Verify solution: {step3}\nCheck for errors:"
        step4 = self.llm.predict(verify_prompt)
        steps.append(("Verification", step4))

        return {
            "steps": steps,
            "final_answer": step4
        }
```

**Tree of Thoughts Implementation:**
```python
from typing import List, Tuple

-- Organizations table
CREATE TABLE organizations (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  config JSONB DEFAULT '{}'
);

-- Agents table
CREATE TABLE agents (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  organization_id UUID REFERENCES organizations(id),
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  role TEXT NOT NULL,
  capabilities TEXT[],
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'active',
  config JSONB DEFAULT '{}'
);

-- Tasks table
CREATE TABLE tasks (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  organization_id UUID REFERENCES organizations(id),
  assigned_agent_id UUID REFERENCES agents(id),
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',
  priority INTEGER DEFAULT 5,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  result JSONB
);

-- Decisions table
CREATE TABLE decisions (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  organization_id UUID REFERENCES organizations(id),
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  options JSONB NOT NULL,
  votes JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'open',
  deadline TIMESTAMPTZ,
  result JSONB
);

-- Enable RLS
ALTER TABLE organizations ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE agents ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE tasks ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

tier4-integrations_3363.content:
**Integration Type:** GPT Fine-Tuning with Reflection

**Overview:**
Implementation of reflection-tuning methodology for fine-tuning GPT models. Uses special tokens (`<thinking>`, `<reflection>`, `<output>`) to train models with explicit reasoning chains.

tier4-integrations_3392.title: API Structure
tier4-integrations_3392.content:
**Reflection Training Data Format:**
```python
REFLECTION_FORMAT = """<thinking>
{thinking_process}
</thinking>

<reflection>
{self_critique}
</reflection>

<output>
{final_answer}
</output>"""

def create_training_example(prompt: str, response: dict) -> dict:
    """Create a reflection-formatted training example."""
    formatted_response = REFLECTION_FORMAT.format(
        thinking_process=response['thinking'],
        self_critique=response['reflection'],
        final_answer=response['output']
    )

    return {
        "prompt": prompt,
        "completion": formatted_response
    }
```

**Fine-Tuning Pipeline:**
```python
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer, TrainingArguments
from peft import LoraConfig, get_peft_model
from datasets import load_dataset

class ReflectionTrainer:
    def __init__(self, base_model: str = "microsoft/phi-2"):
        self.tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(base_model)
        self.model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
            base_model,
            load_in_8bit=True,
            device_map="auto"
        )

        # Add special tokens
        special_tokens = {
            "additional_special_tokens": [
                "<thinking>", "</thinking>",
                "<reflection>", "</reflection>",
                "<output>", "</output>"
            ]
        }
        self.tokenizer.add_special_tokens(special_tokens)
        self.model.resize_token_embeddings(len(self.tokenizer))

        # Configure LoRA
        lora_config = LoraConfig(
            r=16,
            lora_alpha=32,
            target_modules=["q_proj", "v_proj"],
            lora_dropout=0.05,
            bias="none",
            task_type="CAUSAL_LM"
        )

        self.model = get_peft_model(self.model, lora_config)

    def prepare_dataset(self, data_path: str):
        """Load and prepare training dataset."""
        dataset = load_dataset("json", data_files=data_path)

        def tokenize(example):

tier4-integrations_3501.title: Integration Examples
tier4-integrations_3501.content:
**Generate Reflection Data:**
```python
import openai

def generate_reflection_data(prompts: list, model: str = "gpt-4") -> list:
    """Generate reflection-style training data using GPT-4."""
    training_data = []

    system_prompt = """You are a helpful assistant that thinks step by step.

    For each response, use this format:
    <thinking>Your reasoning process</thinking>
    <reflection>Self-critique and verification</reflection>
    <output>Final answer</output>"""

    for prompt in prompts:
        response = openai.ChatCompletion.create(
            model=model,
            messages=[
                {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}
            ]
        )

        training_data.append({
            "prompt": prompt,
            "completion": response.choices[0].message.content
        })

    return training_data

tier4-integrations_3534.title: Generate and save training data
tier4-integrations_3534.content:
prompts = [
    "Explain quantum entanglement",
    "How does photosynthesis work?",
    "What causes inflation?"
]

data = generate_reflection_data(prompts)

import json
with open("reflection_data.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
```

**Inference with Reflection:**
```python
def generate_with_reflection(model, tokenizer, prompt: str) -> dict:
    """Generate response with reflection parsing."""
    inputs = tokenizer(prompt, return_tensors="pt").to(model.device)

    outputs = model.generate(
        **inputs,
        max_new_tokens=1024,
        temperature=0.7,
        do_sample=True,
        pad_token_id=tokenizer.eos_token_id
    )

    response = tokenizer.decode(outputs[0], skip_special_tokens=False)

    # Parse reflection components
    import re

    thinking = re.search(r'<thinking>(.*?)</thinking>', response, re.DOTALL)
    reflection = re.search(r'<reflection>(.*?)</reflection>', response, re.DOTALL)
    output = re.search(r'<output>(.*?)</output>', response, re.DOTALL)

    return {
        "thinking": thinking.group(1).strip() if thinking else "",
        "reflection": reflection.group(1).strip() if reflection else "",
        "output": output.group(1).strip() if output else response
    }
```

**Key Features:**
- Reflection-tuning methodology
- Special token training
- LoRA fine-tuning
- GPT-4 data generation
- Phi-2 base model
- Reasoning chain extraction

tier4-integrations_3928.title: API Structure
tier4-integrations_3928.content:
**Kafka Consumer with AI Processing:**
```python
from confluent_kafka import Consumer, Producer
import openai
import json
import asyncio

class InFlightProcessor:
    def __init__(self, kafka_config: dict, openai_key: str):
        self.consumer = Consumer({
            **kafka_config,
            'group.id': 'inflight-processor',
            'auto.offset.reset': 'latest'
        })

        self.producer = Producer(kafka_config)

        openai.api_key = openai_key

    def subscribe(self, topics: list):
        """Subscribe to Kafka topics."""
        self.consumer.subscribe(topics)

    async def process_event(self, event: dict) -> dict:
        """Process event with AI analysis."""
        response = await openai.ChatCompletion.acreate(
            model="gpt-4",
            messages=[
                {"role": "system", "content": "Analyze this event and provide insights."},
                {"role": "user", "content": json.dumps(event)}
            ],
            stream=True
        )

        analysis = ""
        async for chunk in response:
            if chunk.choices[0].delta.content:
                analysis += chunk.choices[0].delta.content

        return {
            "original_event": event,
            "analysis": analysis,
            "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat()
        }

    def publish_result(self, topic: str, result: dict):
        """Publish processed result to Kafka."""
        self.producer.produce(
            topic,
            value=json.dumps(result).encode('utf-8')
        )
        self.producer.flush()

    async def run(self, input_topic: str, output_topic: str):
        """Main processing loop."""
        self.subscribe([input_topic])

        while True:
            msg = self.consumer.poll(1.0)

            if msg is None:
                continue

            if msg.error():
                print(f"Consumer error: {msg.error()}")
                continue

            event = json.loads(msg.value().decode('utf-8'))

tier5-utilities_340.title: aws-dev/lambda/handler.py
tier5-utilities_340.content:
import json
import boto3

def handler(event, context):
    """
    Standard Lambda handler template with:
    - Error handling
    - Logging
    - Response formatting
    """
    try:
        body = json.loads(event.get('body', '{}'))
        result = process_request(body)

        return {
            'statusCode': 200,
            'headers': {'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
            'body': json.dumps(result)
        }
    except Exception as e:
        return {
            'statusCode': 500,
            'body': json.dumps({'error': str(e)})
        }
```

tier5-utilities_369.title: 3.3 Supa-Ruv
tier5-utilities_369.content:
**Purpose**: Supabase integration utilities and templates.

**Key Features**:
- Database schema templates
- Auth configuration helpers
- Real-time subscription utilities
- Edge function templates

**Usage**:
```typescript
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'
import { SupaRuv } from 'supa-ruv'

// Initialize with helpers
const suparuv = new SupaRuv({
  url: process.env.SUPABASE_URL,
  key: process.env.SUPABASE_KEY
})

// Use enhanced queries
const users = await suparuv.query('users')
  .select('*')
  .withPagination(page, limit)
  .withSearch('name', searchTerm)
  .execute()

// Real-time subscriptions with auto-reconnect
suparuv.subscribe('messages', {
  onInsert: (message) => console.log('New:', message),
  onUpdate: (message) => console.log('Updated:', message),
  autoReconnect: true
})
```

**Database Migrations**:
```sql
-- supa-ruv/migrations/001_initial.sql
CREATE TABLE users (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE messages (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Enable RLS
ALTER TABLE users ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE messages ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
```

import { AutoBrowser } from 'auto-browser'

const browser = new AutoBrowser()

// Navigate and interact
await browser.goto('https://example.com')

**Advanced Automation**:
```typescript
// Create reusable flows
const loginFlow = browser.createFlow('login', async (page, credentials) => {
  await page.goto('/login')
  await page.fill('#email', credentials.email)
  await page.fill('#password', credentials.password)
  await page.click('button[type="submit"]')
  await page.waitForNavigation()
})

// Execute flow
await browser.runFlow('login', { email: 'user@test.com', password: 'pass' })
```
